old_value:
{"id":"broadside-r9da.59","title":"delivery.rs: HTTP 202 treated as failure, triggers retry","description":"LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:294-349, process_batch()

FINDING: The success arm only matches 'resp.status().is_success()' (2xx) and the 410 arm. HTTP 202 Accepted is a 2xx status and IS caught by is_success(), so it is handled correctly. However, Mastodon and other AP servers commonly return 202 Accepted, which is within 2xx. This part is fine.

The actual bug is subtler: when an inbox returns any non-2xx, non-410 status (e.g. 429 Too Many Requests, 503, 401), the code calls handle_retry() AND records a circuit-breaker failure. A 429 or 503 with a Retry-After header is treated identically to a permanent 5xx failure — there is no backoff based on the Retry-After header, and repeated 429s count toward the circuit breaker trip threshold of 10. This causes legitimate rate-limited remotes to be circuit-broken after 10 retries rather than respected.

IMPACT: Delivery to busy/rate-limiting servers gets permanently circuit-broken rather than respecting Retry-After, leading to missed deliveries to large instances that rate-limit aggressively.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:55:52.044996113Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T01:55:52.044996113Z","labels":["correctness","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-r9da.59","depends_on_id":"broadside-r9da","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-20T01:55:52.074749983Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"title":"delivery.rs: 429 Too Many Requests triggers circuit breaker instead of respecting Retry-After"}
Fixed: 429 responses handled separately, no longer trip circuit breaker
